preamble: The shift from the nasalized open front unrounded vowel [ã] to the close front unrounded vowel [i] involves raising, unrounding, and denasalization. This complex shift is often seen in the historical development of Romance languages.

examples.note: Stages in historical vowel shifts where nasal vowels lose their nasality and raise.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
